name: App quản lý in ấn
description:
Role: You are a Senior AppSheet Architect and Business Analyst.

Objective: Design a comprehensive database schema and application logic for a Printing & Packaging Workshop Management App.

Context: The user manages a printing business (Offset, Flexo, UV, PET) specializing in clothing labels, tags, and packaging. Pricing is calculated manually in external Excel files. This app will act as the operational "Operating System" to manage orders, workflow, and finances after the price is determined.

1. Data Schema Requirements (Tables & Key Columns):

Customers (KhachHang): CustomerID, Name, Phone, Address, SalesRep (Link to Users), CustomerStatus (Care, Pending, VIP).

Orders (DonHang): OrderID, CustomerID, OrderDate, ItemName, Specifications (Dimensions, PaperType, Color, Lamination, Sides, DieCut, Foiling), Quantity, ProductionMethod (Quick Print/Offset), FinalPrice (User Input from Excel), TotalValue, Deposit (50%), RemainingBalance, CurrentStatus, DesignFile (Link/Image).

ProductionLog (Trạng thái đơn): LogID, OrderID, Stage (Sale Confirmed -> Design -> Printing -> Post-processing -> Packing -> Delivered), UpdatedBy, Timestamp.

Materials (Vật tư): MaterialID, MaterialName (Paper, Satin, Cotton), SupplierID, StockQuantity, Unit, Category.

Accounting (Công nợ): TransactionID, EntityType (Customer/Supplier/Subcontractor), EntityID, Amount, Type (Income/Expense), Status (Paid/Unpaid), RelatedOrderID.

Users (Nguời dùng): UserEmail, UserName, Role (Admin, Sale, Production), AssignedDept.

2. Key Business Logic & Workflows:

Order Workflow: Sales enter order details and the final price from the Excel calculator. Status updates trigger notifications to the next department (e.g., Design confirms -> Printing team notified).

Payment Logic: Automatic calculation: [RemainingBalance] = [TotalValue] - [Deposit]. Highlight orders that are "Delivered" but have [RemainingBalance] > 0.

Inventory Link: When an order is "Printing", allow the user to deduct materials (e.g., Paper, Ribbon) from the Materials table.

3. Security & Access Control (Crucial):

Sales Role: Can view all customers to avoid duplicates. Can see Order Debt. STRICTLY PROHIBITED from viewing "Cost Price" (Gia Von) or "Profit Margin" columns. Can only see their own assigned orders or general order status.

Admin Role: Full access to financial reports, profit margins, and supplier debt.

Production Role: Can only see technical specifications and update the "CurrentStatus".

4. Automation & Output:

Export to Excel: Create an automation to generate "Quotations" (Báo giá) and "Delivery Notes" (Phiếu giao hàng) based on an Excel Template.

Price History: If a price is adjusted manually after being entered, the app must log the previous value and the reason for change in a "PriceLog" table to prevent errors in accounting.

5. Dashboard & Reporting Requirements:

Order Volume & Profitability (Admin only).

Unpaid Orders List (Aged Receivables).

Production Bottleneck Chart (Orders stuck in specific stages).

Follow-up List for customers needing care.

Deliverables:

Provide a detailed Table Structure with Column Names and Column Types (Ref, Price, Enum, etc.).

Provide AppSheet Virtual Column formulas for calculating debt and status.

Define the Security Filters for the Sales role.

Explain how to set up the Automation to export data to the user's Excel template.
